title: eriophorum × beringianum
intro: Eriophorum × beringianum, also known as Bering's cotton-grass or Bering's cotton sedge, is a hybrid plant species within the Eriophorum genus. It is known for its white, cotton-like seeds that resemble a fluffy tuft, which are often used in herbal medicine. Eriophorum × beringianum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its seeds can be used to make a poultice or infused oil for topical application.
